stories.title: Trump may be toxic and Orbán is gone, but Europe’s far right is not in decline | Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Let’s not draw the wrong conclusions from Hungary’s election or the US president’s troubles Viktor Orbán’s crushing defeat in last month’s Hungarian election has led to an outbreak of democratic optimism. Across the globe, democrats are drawing lessons from the results and speculating about the decline of the far right . There is simultaneously a consensus that Donald Trump has gone from inspiration to “ liability ” for the global far right. While the fall of Orbán has great symbolic significanc

stories.title: Hungary, EU split over how much of its €10B funding it should get
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar is at loggerheads with the European Commission in the first real test of the post-Viktor Orbán reset: How much of Budapest’s frozen EU funds can he bring home? Over the past few days, talks have intensified over the €10.4 billion Hungary is entitled to under the EU’s post-pandemic recovery fund, according to two EU and one Hungarian official. The money had been withheld over Budapest’s breaches of EU law under Orbán, w

stories.title: JM Coetzee declines to attend Jerusalem writers festival over Israel’s ‘genocidal campaign in Gaza’
stories.summary: Nobel laureate says he previously considered himself a supporter of Israel, but ‘the campaign of annihilation in Gaza has changed all that’ Nobel laureate JM Coetzee has declined to attend an upcoming literature festival in Israel, writing a excoriating letter to organisers citing the country’s “genocidal campaign” in Gaza, stating: “It will take many years for Israel to clear its name”. The 86-year-old author, who was born in apartheid South Africa and lives in Australia, wrote to organisers of

stories.title: ‘People should be talking about it’: moves to curtail vaccine information obscures important science, doctors say
stories.summary: Quashed studies, halted publications and canceled research threaten damage to public health, critics say Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A series of high-profile and under-the-radar decisions by US health agencies have scientists and doctors questioning the extent of the agencies’ control over public communications – and they say the debate is obscuring the most important part, which is informing the public about key updates in science and medicine . Studies on the safety of va

stories.title: World Health Organization expects more hantavirus cases but doesn’t ‘anticipate large epidemic’ - Europe live
stories.summary: The outbreak has been linked to a birdwatching expedition in Argentina joined by two passengers before they boarded the ship Meanwhile, Hungary’s incoming prime minister Péter Magyar has arrived for talks at Palazzo Chigi in Rome, where he is due to meet with Italy’s prime minister Giorgia Meloni. Since he is not the Hungarian PM yet – he will take his role this weekend – he was officially welcomed outside the Palazzo by a senior adviser to the Italian PM instead. Continue reading...

stories.title: Eltérített egy helikoptert, szétosztotta a vagyonát, és még a családját is kirabolta az úri családból származó elitellenes harcos
stories.summary: Amikor Rose Dugdale 1941-ben megszületett egy gazdag angol családban, minden arra utalt, hogy az élete a brit felső osztálybeliek megszokott útját követi majd. Hát nem így lett.

stories.title: Megszünteti a TV2 a Tényeket
stories.summary: A tv elnök-vezérigazgatója gyakorlatilag elismerte, hogy nem a szakmai sztenderdeknek megfelelő hírszolgáltatást nyújtott a csatorna.

stories.title: Navracsics a Fidesz bukásának okairól: Mi egész egyszerűen leértékeltük a középosztályt
stories.summary: Navracsics Tibor abban bízik, hogy Orbán Viktor továbbra is megkerülhetetlen szereplője lesz a magyar politikának.

stories.title: A magyar média valaha volt leghitványabb műsorának nevezte Magyar Péter a megszűnés előtt álló Tényeket
stories.summary: Egy banános fotóval üzent „a fideszes öntökönszúrás koronázatlan bajnokainak” a Tisza Párt elnöke, akiről egy igazán emlékezetes lejárató anyagot is készített a TV2.

stories.title: GKI: Másfélszer annyi üzemanyagra elég az uniós átlagkereset, mint a magyar
stories.summary: Régen nagyobb volt a különbség, de egyrészt szépen nőttek a hazai bérek, másrészt a védett ár miatt nálunk most eleve mesterséges, államilag szabályozott „jóárasítás” torzítja a piacot.

stories.title: Lázár János érvénytelenítette a Pázmány Campus tenderét
stories.summary: A kormány 2020-ban döntött arról, hogy a korábban Piliscsabán működő egyetem Budapest belvárosába költözhet. A bíróság februárban felfüggesztette az építési engedélyt. A beruházás ellen évek óta tiltakoznak a józsefvárosiak, Pikó András polgármester szerint öt és féléves jogtiprás ért most véget.

stories.title: Drónok zuhantak le Lettországban, olajtartályok rongálódtak meg
stories.summary: A lett védelmi miniszter szerint a drónokat valószínűleg Ukrajna indította oroszországi célpontok ellen.

stories.title: Budapesten született az orosz GRU titkos hekkerképző elitiskolájának egyik oktatója
stories.summary: Apja a Magyarországot megszálló Szovjetunió Déli Hadseregcsoportjában szolgált.

stories.title: Average EU income buys one and a half times as much fuel as average Hungarian income, analysis finds
stories.summary: The gap used to be wider, but on the one hand, Hungarian wages have risen steadily, and on the other hand, the market is distorted by the artificial, state-regulated “price ceiling.”

stories.title: Már egyetlen adagnyi varázsgomba is anatómiai változásokat indíthat el az agyban
stories.summary: Egy új kutatás segíthet megmagyarázni, hogy miért hatásosak a pszichedelikumok például a szorongás vagy a depresszió kezelésében.

stories.title: Egy ajánlat, ami feladja a leckét a többi SUV-nak (x)
stories.summary: A számok nem hazudnak. Aki körülnéz a budapesti forgalomban, vagy végigsétál egy nagyobb bevásárlóközpont parkolójában, pontosan látja, merre tart az autópiac. A C-SUV kategória továbbra is az egyik legnépszerűbb választás Magyarországon, és ebben a mezőnyben van egy modell, amely évek óta stabilan tartja vezető pozícióját: a Nissan Qashqai.

stories.title: Jubileumi évad, rekordszámú program: X. Műtárgyak Éjszakája Fesztivál (x)
stories.summary: 2026. május 14. és 16. között, 70 fővárosi helyszínen több mint 120 programmal rendezik meg a Műtárgyak Éjszakája Fesztivált, amely nélkül ma már elképzelhetetlen lenne Budapest tavaszi kulturális-művészeti programkínálata.

stories.title: Női-férfi ügyvezető páros és történelmi beruházás a Rossmannál (x)
stories.summary: 2026. július 1-től Szimeiszter Éva és Váradi István közösen vezetik a Rossmann Magyarországot.

stories.title: Instructor at Russia's secret elite hacker training school was born in Budapest
stories.summary: His father served in the Soviet Southern Group of Forces, which were stationed in Hungary until 1991.

stories.title: ECFR: A magyarok több mint fele nem akar leválni az orosz energiáról
stories.summary: A közvélemény-kutatás szerint a magyarok belpolitikai reformokat várnak Magyar Pétertől, és megosztottak Ukrajna kérdésében.

stories.title: Már menthetetlen lehet az EU helyreállítási alapjának egy része
stories.summary: A Politicónak és a Népszavának is arra utaltak uniós források, hogy túl kevésnek tűnik az idő az Orbán-kormány lemaradásának behozására.

stories.title: Magyar Péter egyeztetett Giorgia Melonival, szóba került a trieszti magyar kikötő is
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök azt írta, sikeres és előremutató megbeszélést folytatott az olasz miniszterelnökkel, akit meg is hívott Magyarországra.

stories.title: Felfoghatatlan összegek nehezen értelmezhető projektekre – NKA-képbehozó
stories.summary: Mága Zoltán fotósa mégis mire kapott 500 milliót? Baán László és Vidnyánszky Attila tényleg nem látta az általuk jóváhagyott beszámolót? És ki az a Krucsainé Herter Anikó? Nem csodáljuk, ha kezd elveszni a Nemzeti Kulturális Alap körüli botrány részleteiben. Képbehozó cikkünkben nemcsak a fideszes celebeknek és szervezeteknek titokban kiszórt 17 milliárd útját meséljük el, de elmagyarázzuk a szervezet működésének alapjait is.

stories.title: ECFR: More than half of Hungarians reluctant to quit Russian energy
stories.summary: The survey found that most Hungarians expect Péter Magyar to carry out reform at home, and are divided on the subject of Ukraine.

stories.title: Reagált a külügy Palkovics kijelentésére, miszerint a kormányüléseken többször tárgyalták az akkugyárak szennyezéseit
stories.summary: A KKM szerint ez nem áll ellentmondásban Szijjártó korábbi kijelentéseivel.

stories.title: Péter Magyar met with Giorgia Meloni, the Hungarian port in Trieste among subjects discussed
stories.summary: The incoming Hungarian PM described the talks as successful and forward-looking, and said he had invited the Italian leader to Hungary.

stories.title: Telex Bécsben – Választások után, rendszerváltás előtt
stories.summary: Merre tovább, Magyarország? Optimistább a hangulat, milyen irányba mutat az alakuló kormány kommunikációja? Erről is beszélgetünk Bécsben május 26-án.

stories.title: Magyarországon a legmagasabb a fogvatartottak aránya az EU-ban
stories.summary: Az Eurostat szerint 2024-ben az uniós tagállamok többségében enyhén emelkedett a fogvatartottak száma az előző évhez képest.

stories.title: Aprópénzből csináltak BEK-győztes csapatot
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. A 35 évvel ezelőtt Bariban rendezett meccsel és a Crvena zvezda győzelmével kezdjük. BL-sztorik, első rész.

stories.title: Does the name of Hungary’s next PM really translate to Peter Hungarian?
stories.summary: In short: yes. And although his party’s election victory a few weeks ago was truly unprecedented, his last name is not nearly as unique as it may sound to foreign ears.

stories.title: Elfogták a Magyar Nemzeti Vagyonkezelő volt főigazgatóját, őrizetbe is vették
stories.summary: Két és fél milliárd forintos hűtlen kezelés megalapozott gyanúja miatt hallgatták ki.

stories.title: Hungary's new government pushes for euro by 2030
stories.summary: Hungary's new leadership wants the country to adopt the euro by the end of the decade while repairing strained ties with Brussels. But with a weak economy and tight deadlines, experts warn the path will be steep.

stories.title: Romania's government falls, far-right firewall crumbles
stories.summary: Romania's Social Democrats have joined forces with the far right to oust the pro-reform government of Ilie Bolojan. The economic impact will be serious; the political cost high. Is the far right on the brink of power?

stories.title: Romania's government collapses after Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan loses confidence vote
stories.summary: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan has been voted out of office by lawmakers. Romania's coalition was driven apart by unpopular austerity measures, with the Social Democrats leaving and moving to oust the PM.

stories.title: Spanish government under fire over handling of hantavirus ship
stories.summary: The Spanish government is facing criticism for sending mixed signals over how it plans to handle passengers of a virus-stricken cruise due to dock in the Canary Islands this weekend. Over the past 24 hours, authorities in Madrid have issued mixed messages on how the country intends to process the 147 passengers travelling aboard the MV Hondius, the cruise ship on which a hantavirus outbreak has already resulted in the death of three travelers. While there is consensus that all non-native asympto

stories.title: Lawmakers, industry push EU to move faster on AI rule cuts
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU should have gone further in cutting rules for artificial intelligence under a deal struck Thursday, some lawmakers and industry lobby groups said. The reaction from liberal and centrist politicians suggests a strong appetite for further changes to the bloc’s digital laws — satisfying demand from both industry and the U.S. government for further deregulation. The deal agreed by EU legislators on Thursday morning includes plans to delay rules on high-risk uses of AI as part of an

stories.title: China hands 2 former defense ministers suspended death sentences
stories.summary: A Chinese military court has handed two former defense ministers suspended death sentences following their convictions on graft charges. Wei Fenghe, who served as defense minister from 2018 to 2023, and his successor Li Shangfu, who held the post for several months in 2023, were convicted on Thursday of accepting bribes. Li was also found guilty of offering bribes. Both were given a suspended death sentence with a two-year reprieve and no parole, which is often automatically commuted to life imp

stories.title: Woman jailed in Somalia for peaceful protest ‘stripped, kicked and beaten’
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview from prison, Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old rickshaw driver, tells of her treatment after being arrested for demonstrating against the government A woman being held in prison in Somalia for taking part in peaceful protests has described how she was tortured by her guards. Sadia Moalim Ali, 27, told the Guardian she was stripped naked by two male guards in a room monitored by CCTV, kicked, beaten with a baton and left for two days in a small cell without food. Continue

stories.title: Trump’s tantrums over Nato are prompting European leaders to think the unthinkable | Paul Taylor
stories.summary: Europeans must urgently seek to close their security gaps in case Russia attacks – and the US refuses to defend its allies Donald Trump’s war in Iran and tirades against Nato allies are accelerating moves to develop a plan B for European security in case the US is no longer willing to help defend allies against a Russian attack. Europe must prepare for sudden vulnerability gaps if the fickle US president decides to pull out key military enablers before Europeans can develop their own alternative

stories.title: Doctors’ union drops opposition to Cass review of NHS gender healthcare
stories.summary: British Medical Association says review into gender identity services was ‘robust’ after previously rejecting findings The trade union representing doctors across the UK has dropped its opposition to the findings of the Cass review of gender identity services across the NHS. The British Medical Association (BMA) had previously rejected the findings of the landmark review of transgender healthcare, with the medical body refusing to endorse the report’s findings. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump counterterrorism strategy targets ‘violent left-wing extremists’ with ‘transgender ideology’
stories.summary: “Violent left-wing extremists” and “extremist transgender ideology” are among the most concerning threats facing the country, according to the Trump administration counterterrorism strategy released Wednesday. The strategy, the  first released in President Donald Trump’s second term , underscores how Trump’s priorities differ from predecessors, listing “violent left-wing extremists” as one of its top three focuses, alongside “narcoterrorists” and “Islamist terrorists.” It’s a sharp departur

stories.title: Zelenskyy sends chief negotiator to US for fresh peace talks
stories.summary: KYIV — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has dispatched Kyiv’s chief negotiator to meet with Trump envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner in the U.S., a top Ukrainian official told POLITICO. Details are sketchy so far on the trip by Ukraine’s National Security and Defense Council Secretary Rustem Umerov, which comes as the Moscow-Kyiv peace negotiations mediated by Washington appear to have hit a wall. Umerov left Ukraine for the U.S. on Wednesday, said the official, granted an

stories.title: EU agreement on US trade deal within reach, says top lawmaker
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — An agreement to implement the EU’s trade deal with the United States is on track to be finalized later this month, senior lawmaker Bernd Lange told POLITICO. Lange, the European Parliament’s top trade negotiator, said an intra-EU deal was within reach, even though the EU institutions failed to reach a compromise in talks that stretched into the early hours of Thursday morning. The meeting between negotiators from the European Commission, EU capitals, and the Parliament took place unde

stories.title: Europe’s AI translation industry told it risks reputation by partnering with US firms
stories.summary: Partnership between top startup DeepL and Amazon comes amid concern about Silicon Valley’s monopoly over digital infrastructure AI companies in Europe risk losing their world-leading status in the field of machine translation, industry figures have said, after the decision by one of the continent’s leading startups to partner with Amazon’s cloud computing division provoked alarm. While businesses in the EU have generally lagged behind the US and China in AI adoption, a small group of European co

stories.title: Marco Rubio to meet pope at the Vatican after Trump attacks on pontiff
stories.summary: US secretary of state will hold talks with Italian government, also berated by Trump for not supporting Iran war The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, is to meet Pope Leo at the Vatican on Thursday in an effort to ease tensions after Donald Trump’s repeated criticisms of the first North American pontiff. Amid unprecedented strain on relations between the Holy See and Washington, Rubio is expected to meet Leo at the Apostolic Palace in the morning, before holding a series of meetings with the I

stories.title: More than 50,000 pupils expected to strike over German rearmament policy
stories.summary: Organisers of nationwide protest say its aim is to stop the government turning young Germans into ‘cannon fodder’ Tens of thousands of pupils across Germany are expected to boycott the classroom and take to the streets in a nationwide protest organisers say is to stop the government’s rearmament policy turning young people into “cannon fodder”. Despite threats from teachers’ associations and education ministries, which have said anyone who demonstrates during school hours could risk penalties an

stories.title: May elections live: Badenoch rows back on Reform pacts as millions cast their votes
stories.summary: Tory leader tells Sun that there won’t be council tie-ups as Farage’s party is not ‘serious’ as votes under way in England, Wales and Scotland And here is the eve-of-poll statement that Rhun ap Iorwerth , the Plaid Cymru leader, issued yesterday. Service is a value which has always sustained Wales. It’s a value instilled in me from a young age by my parents, both teachers. It’s a value I’ve sought to pass on to my children - the gift of giving back to the people and places who gave us so much. T

stories.title: Woman charged with joining Islamic State after arriving back in Australia
stories.summary: Two more women arrested and expected to be charged after a group of 13 with family links to IS landed back from Syria on Thursday Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A woman who arrived back in Australia after spending more than seven years in Syrian detention camps has been charged in Sydney with allegedly entering a declared conflict zone and joining Islamic State. stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran reviewing peace proposal as Trump says a deal ‘very possible’
stories.summary: The US and Iran have offered conflicting messages over the likelihood of a deal being reached imminently The European Union is set to tell airlines the impact from the Iran war on tourism is not yet severe enough to justify emergency measures for the sector, draft EU guidelines seen by Reuters showed. “The current situation does not point to the need for dedicated measures for the tourism sector, unlike during the COVID-19 crisis,” said the draft EU guidelines, which the European Commission is d

stories.title: Susan Collins reveals medical condition but says her job as senator is unaffected
stories.summary: Republican senator for Maine, facing tough re-election battle, says she has had benign essential tremor for decades US politics live – latest updates Susan Collins, the Republican Maine senator seen by Democrats as vulnerable in November’s midterm election, has revealed a decades-old medical condition she said affects her appearance, but not her ability to do her job. Collins, 73, told News Center Maine , an NBC affiliate, that she has a benign essential tremor she treats with medication, which

stories.title: Authorities search home connected to 1996 killing of college student Kristin Smart
stories.summary: Smart went missing in California after returning from a party, and was declared legally dead in 2002 Authorities plan Thursday to enter the second day of their search of a home connected to the man convicted of killing 19-year-old college student Kristin Smart in 1996, according to law enforcement. The San Luis Obispo county sheriff’s office served the warrant Wednesday in the continuing investigation into Smart’s disappearance. Her remains were never found and she was declared legally dead in 2

stories.title: Lib Dems accuse Badenoch of being willing to ‘put Farage in No 10’ after she hints she would approve council pacts – as it happened
stories.summary: Conservative leader says her party ‘are willing to work with people who will help deliver Conservative policies’ Labour has criticised Zack Polanski for saying he was concerned about how the suspect in the Golders Green stabbings was treated when he was arrested by the police. Referring to what Polanski said about this in his Today interview this morning (see 9.33am ), a Labour party spokesperson said: Our brave police ran towards a suspected terrorist and tackled him while he was still carrying

stories.title: Is Russian oil becoming a lifeline for Southeast Asia?
stories.summary: With oil, gas and fertilizer imports squeezed by the Iran war, Southeast Asian governments are brushing off EU pressure and treating Russian energy as a tool of economic survival.

stories.title: Austria: Police report 3 dead in shooting in Linz, say no further danger to public
stories.summary: Police in Austria report that three people have died after gunfire was heard in the city. Local media reported a large police presence in the north of the city. Police also said there was no ongoing threat to the public.

stories.title: Australia: 3 women with IS links arrested on return home
stories.summary: The women flew back to Australia from Syria after years in a refugee camp. Children were also among the returnees.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike kills 4 and injures 33 in southern Lebanon despite fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: Israeli airstrike hits house in Saksakieh despite ceasefire, killing four and injuring 33, as raids in southern Lebanon stoke fears of renewed escalation.

stories.title: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire, reportedly killing Hezbollah commander
stories.summary: Beirut and its southern suburbs had been spared Israeli attacks since 8 April, when massive Israeli strikes across the country killed more than 350 people.

stories.title: Iranians at home divided between hope for regime change and war fears
stories.summary: People in Iran who spoke to Euronews said they were torn between hope for regime change and fear of devastation, while reflecting on US-Israeli strikes, rising hardship and fading support for war.

stories.title: Russia strikes Kharkiv residential district as drone attacks escalate
stories.summary: A major Russian drone offensive struck Kharkiv’s Novobavarskyi district and other cities, injuring civilians and destroying homes despite Ukraine’s strong air defence.

stories.title: Three women linked to Islamic State arrested in Australia on return from Syria
stories.summary: Three women who allegedly supported IS were arrested after they flew home to Australia for the first time in years.

stories.title: China announces suspended death sentences for former defence ministers
stories.summary: The announcement follows recent ousting of several top officials in China's military.

stories.title: US underlines 'strong relationship' with Vatican as Rubio meets pope after Trump criticism
stories.summary: Rubio's visit comes after US President Trump accused the pope of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy" after he made critical comments about the Iran war.

stories.title: EU body drops plan for €3.6M conference hall
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — A controversial €3.6 million plan to build a 450-seat conference hall at the Committee of the Regions’ Brussels headquarters has been put on hold. CoR President Kata Tüttő told POLITICO Brussels Playbook the proposal is “off the agenda” while political leaders assess its viability. Politicians on the CoR became aware of the plans, drawn up by the institution’s administration, only when they were presented to them for approval. “Now it’s off the table because all of us felt that we nee

stories.title: ‘Came here with hope’ and ‘died unseen’: tearful crowd says goodbye to homeless man Bikram Lama
stories.summary: People gathered at dusk under the fig tree canopy of Sydney’s Hyde Park to pay tribute to the 32-year-old who died nearby and lay undiscovered for up to a week Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast No one should be left to die in the middle of Sydney, alone and unseen. That was the powerful message delivered by homelessness support worker Erin Longbottom to a crowd gathered in Hyde Park to honour Bikram Lama. Continue

stories.title: ‘At a crossroads’: will piling-up crises force Europe to put brakes on SUV culture?
stories.summary: Bigger cars including electric can cause multiple harms, yet resistance to rise of US-style vehicles has had mixed support On a brisk winter’s evening in Europe’s automotive heartland, a cyclist who had pushed for safer streets went out on his bike for a final time. Andreas Mandalka had documented dangerous driving and shoddy cycling infrastructure for years, measuring the margins at which cars zipped past him and posting videos of blatant violations. stories.title: Up to 2cm a month: Nasa keeps track as Mexico City sinks into the ground
stories.summary: Powerful radar system is providing new data on city’s subsidence, which experts hope will draw more attention to it Walking into Mexico City’s sprawling central Zócalo is a dizzying experience. At one end of the plaza, the capital’s cathedral, with its soaring spires, slumps in one direction. An attached church, known as the Metropolitan Sanctuary, tilts in the other. The nearby National Palace also seems off-kilter. The teetering of many of the capital’s historic buildings is the most visible s

stories.title: Reopening strait of Hormuz would have limited impact on cargo flows, says Maersk
stories.summary: CEO of Danish shipping group says increased costs due to higher fuel bills passed on to customers Business live – latest updates The boss of the shipping company Maersk has said the reopening of the strait of Hormuz would have a “limited impact” on cargo flows, as the industry grapples with a sharp rise in energy costs. stories.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby: Advocates say five-year-old’s death ‘should not be politicised’ as hundreds gather at vigils
stories.summary: Crowds were invited to stand in silent solidarity with the family of the Warlpiri girl who was allegedly murdered in Alice Springs last week Warning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The national body representing First Nations children has called for an end to the politicisation of the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, as hundreds of people gathered around the country to mourn her passing. Communi

stories.title: ‘Restaurants won’t survive’: Michelin chef opens venues abroad to withstand UK taxes
stories.summary: Jason Atherton, who has restaurants in Dubai, St Moritz and now Tuscany, says it’s tough to stay afloat in UK hospitality industry A British Michelin-starred chef says he is opening restaurants abroad to subsidise his UK venues against a backdrop of high taxes and a struggling hospitality sector. Jason Atherton is now in Forte dei Marmi, on the Tuscan coast in Italy, where he is preparing his newest opening, Maria’s, which will be in the Principessa hotel. The Sheffield-born chef now has restaur

stories.title: German tourist awarded €1,000 after losing out on sunloungers at Greek hotel
stories.summary: Court in Hanover says man entitled to payout after he and his family had to lie on concrete A German holidaymaker has won a payout of almost €1,000 after being unable to find a sunlounger for himself and his family because other guests had got there first. The man, whose identity is not known, holidayed on the island of Kos, in Greece, with his family in 2024. He said that, despite waking up at the crack of dawn every morning to carry out a 20-minute search, he had not been able to lay claim to

stories.title: Rachel Entrekin becomes first woman to win Cocodona 250 ultramarathon – and pets dogs along the way
stories.summary: 34-year-old heads elite field of men and women American takes small naps during 56-hour run Rachel Entrekin has made history by beating a field of elite men and women to win the Cocodona 250 ultramarathon . Entrekin had won the women’s race on two occasions but on Wednesday she headed the entire field, winning in a record time of 56 hours 9 minutes and 48 seconds. Continue reading...

stories.title: The Only Way Is Essex star Jake Hall found dead in Mallorca
stories.summary: Towie star Hall, 35, found unresponsive with head wounds reportedly caused by shards of glass at villa on Spanish island The Only Way Is Essex star Jake Hall has been found dead in Mallorca. The former reality TV show personality was found unresponsive with head wounds reportedly caused by shards of glass at a villa on the Spanish island. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran conducting near-daily prisoner executions in secrecy, say rights groups
stories.summary: Families pressured to stay silent, with at least 24 executions reportedly taking place since March amid internet blackout Middle East crisis – live updates Iran is carrying out near-daily executions of prisoners in secrecy and, in some cases, refusing to hand the bodies of the dead to their families, according to rights groups and sources close to the relatives of the dead. Many families only learn of executions after they have been carried out, with some facing harassment and pressure not to sp

stories.title: Revealed: owner of former WH Smith stores is charging fee to use fictitious ‘family’ brand
stories.summary: Exclusive: Documents show Modella Capital, which bought the chain last year, is so far owed £2.9m in royalty fees The investment company that owns the former WH Smith high street stores is charging the retailer millions of pounds in licence fees for the right to use its widely derided TG Jones name, the Guardian can reveal. Modella Capital – which bought the chain from WH Smith’s parent company last year – on Wednesday blamed weak consumer spending as it laid out a restructuring plan that could

stories.title: Superdry co-founder James Holder jailed for eight years for raping woman
stories.summary: Holder went back to woman’s Cheltenham flat after night out and refused to stop assault even when she began crying A co-founder of the clothing company Superdry has been jailed for eight years for raping a woman after a night out in Cheltenham. James Holder, 54, had been due to get a taxi back to his home in the Cotswolds with a male friend. Instead, the pair got into the victim’s taxi and went to her flat in the Gloucestershire town, where Holder raped her. Information and support for anyone af

stories.title: Republicans poised to vote on new House map that would erase Democratic seat in Tennessee – US politics live
stories.summary: Such a move could reshape the electoral map ahead of the midterms by carving up a majority-Black congressional district Sign up for the Breaking News US email Susan Collins also revealed a decades-old medical condition she said affects her appearance, but not her ability to do her job. Collins, 73, told News Center Maine , an NBC affiliate, this week that she has a benign essential tremor she treats with medication, which sometimes causes her hands, arms and head to tremble. stories.summary: The 16-page report was led by Trump-ally Sebastian Gorka, and places drug cartels in the Americas at the centre of counter-terrorism efforts The Trump administration has accused Europe of being an “incubator” for terrorism fuelled by mass migration, in a new counter-terrorism strategy unveiled on Wednesday. stories.title: US: New York judge makes alleged Esptein suicide note public
stories.summary: The handwritten document was reportedly found after late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein's first suicide attempt. It could not be verified if the note was authored by him.

stories.title: Celebrated broadcaster and naturalist David Attenborough turns 100
stories.summary: The still-sprightly centenarian has been knighted twice, nominated for the 2022 Nobel Peace Prize, and is perhaps the world's most recognizable nature lover.

stories.title: One year after India-Pakistan conflict, rival narratives persist
stories.summary: On the anniversary of the India-Pakistan conflict, New Delhi and Islamabad remain divided over blame and Trump's role. Meanwhile, Modi said Operation Sindoor showcased India's resolve against terrorism. DW has more.

stories.title: UK votes in key local and regional elections
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party is expected to face major losses, perhaps spelling the end of his premiership. Reform, the Greens, Plaid Cymru and the SNP are all hoping to make gains.

stories.title: 'Dark patterns': Investigating online manipulation
stories.summary: Ireland's media regulator is investigating Facebook and Instagram on suspicion that so-called dark patterns are used to manipulate users.

stories.title: EU reaches tentative deal on simpler AI rules, plans ban on 'nudifier' apps
stories.summary: In a provisional deal, the European Union has agreed on a ban on the use of AI for creating pornographic deepfakes and sexualized imagery. It has also agreed on amending certain rules within the EU's AI Act.

stories.title: Germany news: Coalition at odds over pay rises for MPs
stories.summary: German conservatives want to hold on to automatic pay increases for lawmakers, while the center-left thinks they should skip it this year in light of the economic situation. DW has more.

stories.title: Solar sabotage: Why Europe sees security risks in solar power
stories.summary: Europe's solar boom is powered by Chinese technology. But experts warn Chinese-made tech could threaten the continent's safety and even create blackouts. Now, Brussels is moving to reduce its dependence.

stories.title: Hantavirus: Cruise company says no symptomatic guests on ship
stories.summary: The company that operates the cruise ship struck by the hantavirus said that no passengers showing symptoms have remained on board. It also said 30 guests had disembarked on Saint Helena on April 24. DW has more.

stories.title: Eurovision 2026: Politics collide with a blockbuster show
stories.summary: Controversy over Israel's participation and boycotts unprecedented in scale are already weighing on the Eurovision Song Contest before it even kicks off in Vienna.

stories.title: Marco Rubio meets Pope Leo at the Vatican
stories.summary: The visit by the US secretary of state to the Vatican comes as President Donald Trump continues to disparage the Catholic leader over criticism of the Iran war.

stories.title: Myanmar: What's behind Suu Kyi's transfer to house arrest?
stories.summary: The transfer of Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest has been welcomed cautiously abroad, but experts say Myanmar's military may be using it to boost legitimacy as the nation remains engulfed in civil war.

stories.title: Singapore introduces caning for boys who bully others at school
stories.summary: Under new guidelines caning will only be used in schools for male students aged nine and above Male school students who bully others, including through cyberbullying, will face caning as a “last resort” under new guidelines introduced in Singapore. Male students can face up to three strokes of the cane under the new rules, which were discussed in parliament on Tuesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Three evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship as Spain says vessel can dock

He was named on Wednesday evening as Martin Anstee, 56. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Owner of Japan nightclub says ‘This is a response to a year of insults directed at us – as a country – by the United States’ A Mexico City nightclub has gone viral for charging Americans a nearly $300 cover charge, while citizens from any other country pay just $20 for access, and Mexicans and other Latin Americans pay only $14. The Instagram announcement from the nightclub Japan in the Roma Norte neighborhood has been liked over 26,000 times and received more than 200 comments, mostly supportin

stories.title: ‘Defeated by conspiracy’: West Bengal chief minister refuses to resign after election loss
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee says Narendra Modi’s party ‘forcefully captured’ election that ended her party’s 15 years in power A political showdown is taking place in the Indian state of West Bengal as the chief minister, Mamata Banerjee, India’s most powerful female politician, has refused to resign after she lost elections to the prime minister’s party this week. Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) won an overwhelming victory on Monday in state elections in West Bengal, where Banerjee and her Tri

stories.title: Germany prepares for mission in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The German minesweeper Fulda has set course for the Mediterranean. The German armed forces may assist in clearing mines in the Strait of Hormuz — although only under certain conditions.

stories.title: Germany: Who is CDU parliamentary leader Jens Spahn?
stories.summary: CDU firebrand, ex-health minister, and leader of his party's parliamentary group, Jens Spahn has been dogged by controversy. But his ambition is undimmed.

stories.title: Armenia balances on the tightrope between Russia and the EU
stories.summary: Armenia is moving closer to the EU politically and strategically. But its clear orientation toward the West while keeping close ties with Russia remains a balancing act.

stories.title: Here's what happens when cities kick out cars
stories.summary: A citizen-led campaign in Berlin is collecting signatures to create a car‑free center in the German capital. Other European cities show what happens when streets are built for people.

stories.title: How Rosalia became a global pop force
stories.summary: From a small town in Spain to sold-out global arenas, Spanish singer Rosalia has built her career with precision rather than luck. How did she do it?

stories.title: What the Strait of Hormuz fuel crisis means for air travel
stories.summary: Does travel insurance cover flight cancellations caused by the Iran war? Travelers could face major disruption as the jet fuel crisis threatens summer flights.

stories.title: Venice Biennale in crisis: The controversies explained
stories.summary: Following the resignation of the jury over Russia's and Israel's disputed participation, the traditional Golden Lion will not be awarded this year.

stories.title: How close is the United Kingdom to breaking up?
stories.summary: The May 7 elections could see nationalist gains in Scotland and Wales, while Sinn Fein already leads in Northern Ireland. Is the UK's unity at risk?

stories.title: Germany news: Far-right youth groups raided by police
stories.summary: The groups are considered to be prone to violence and are accused by prosecutors of forming a criminal organization. Meanwhile, Friedrich Merz has completed his first year as chancellor.

stories.title: Russian soldiers rehearse Victory Day parade amid fears of Ukrainian attacks
stories.summary: Russian troops marched through central Moscow during a rehearsal for the upcoming Victory Day parade, which will take place without heavy military equipment.

stories.title: EU's Kallas visits Baku to advance 'strategic partnership' with Azerbaijan
stories.summary: The EU's High Representative Kaja Kallas met Azerbaijan’s leadership in Baku for talks to advance what she called “our strategic interest” in connectivity between the EU, the South Caucasus and Central Asia, and to contribute to the regional peace process.

stories.title: 'I had to stick to the plan': Officer describes retrieving crocodile with human remains inside
stories.summary: DNA tests are underway to confirm whether remains found inside the crocodile match that of a missing man.

stories.title: At least 26 killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine as Zelenskyy denounces Moscow's 'cynicism'
stories.summary: On Monday, Moscow announced a ceasefire during its World War II victory celebrations over 9 May, while Kyiv has said it will halt fire over 6 May.

stories.title: Gulf braces itself for potential escalation with Iran
stories.summary: The Gulf is bracing itself for what could be a major escalation in the Iran war. Attacks against the UAE took place on Monday, the first since the ceasefire was announced in April.

stories.title: Russian attacks kill more than 20 ahead of rival ceasefires proposed by Kyiv and Moscow
stories.summary: Kyiv said it would begin a truce on 6 May and then "act symmetrically" after Moscow declared a pause for its Victory Day parade.

stories.title: BBC traces how 10 minutes of Israeli bombing brought devastation to Lebanon
stories.summary: The bombs started falling at 14:15 in the afternoon - bringing chaos and destruction across the country.

stories.title: India's fiercest female politician faces a fight for survival
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee loses West Bengal state, throwing her party - and political future - into doubt.

stories.title: What is Trump's 'Project Freedom' in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: The US president said the operation has been paused after starting on Monday.

stories.title: Trump renews feud with Pope Leo XIV days ahead of planned Marco Rubio visit to the Vatican
stories.summary: In an interview with US media outlets, Trump said Pope Leo XIV’s words are helping Iran and making the world “less safe”, slamming him for his stance which Trump perceives to mean indifference about Tehran acquiring nuclear weapons.

stories.title: Top Trump advisers urge China to leverage Iran ties to reopen Strait of Hormuz to global traffic
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent have both expressed a need for China to leverage its tight relations with Tehran to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz for “their own interests”.

stories.title: To stay or risk the 'Road of Death' - Ukrainian civilians trapped in frontline city
stories.summary: People in the Ukrainian city of Oleshky say they have been cut off from fresh supplies of food or medicine for months.

stories.title: Russian guided bomb attacks kill at least 26 civilians across Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine said at least 26 people were killed in Russian strikes on Tuesday 5 May 2026, with the heaviest losses in Zaporizhzhia and Kramatorsk.

stories.title: Watch: The Lebanese neighbourhoods shattered by Israel's ten minute bombing raid
stories.summary: The BBC’s Nawal Al-Maghafi has been piecing together what happened on 8 April 2026, after one of the deadliest chapters in the country’s recent history.

stories.title: Mexico marks 164 years since Battle of Puebla with historic reenactment
stories.summary: Mexico marked the 164th anniversary of the Battle of Puebla on Tuesday 5 May 2026, with hundreds of participants recreating the 1862 clash in Mexico City.

stories.title: North Korea drops references to unification with South from constitution, Seoul says
stories.summary: China has long been the country's main economic backer, though Pyongyang has drawn closer to Moscow since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

stories.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard says passage through Strait of Hormuz will be ensured
stories.summary: Earlier on Wednesday, Trump said the US would pause its mission to escort commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz barely two days after it began doing so, citing a desire to reach a peace deal.

stories.title: United Nations demands Israel 'immediately' release two Gaza aid flotilla activists
stories.summary: The flotilla's vessels set sail from France, Spain and Italy with the aim of breaking Israel's blockade of Gaza and delivering humanitarian aid to the war-ravaged Palestinian territory.

stories.title: China calls for Strait to be reopened 'as soon as possible' in Iran talks
stories.summary: Wang Yi is holding talks with Iran's foreign minister Aragchi who is on his first trip to China since the war began.

stories.title: Vivek Ramaswamy wins Republican nomination for Ohio governor
stories.summary: The staunch Trump ally emerged victorious in one of a series of closely-watched primary contests held in the US Midwest.

stories.title: German police raid neo-Nazi criminal youth groups
stories.summary: Police search locations in 12 states, mainly in the East and South of the country in what prosecutors say is a targeted raid.

stories.title: Russia ignores Ukraine's unilateral ceasefire and attacks kindergarten
stories.summary: Ukraine's president says Russia has "spurned the ceasefire" and Kyiv is deciding on further action to take.

stories.title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
stories.summary: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

stories.title: Italy: Femen and Pussy Riot protest Russia’s return to Venice Biennale
stories.summary: The 2026 Venice Biennale opened press previews on Wednesday with protests outside the Russian pavilion, marking Moscow’s return since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine.
